title: Bard Plugin Manifest
description: Frozen contract (root features.md #65) — the declarative seam between a Bard plugin and the console plugin manager. ONE manifest per plugin; it is the only thing the manager needs to browse, enable per-device/per-workgroup, configure, and health-check a plugin WITHOUT the plugin's code being loaded. This is also the worked example in the eds-rules book's capstone chapter (F7 there, Squawk Box): the book documents THIS contract, never the reverse — change here in contracts/ and re-propagate; never patch in a lane or in the book. Kept minimal and honest: only fields a plugin manager actually consumes. requiredCapabilities draws from the same capability vocabulary the Registry advertises and the power-profile schema (power-profile.schema.yaml) keys placement on — a plugin declares what fleet/device capabilities it needs, and the manager only offers it on devices/workgroups that satisfy them.

id.description: Stable identity. Reverse-DNS (pro.bardllm.squawk-box) or a plain kebab id (squawk-box). Lowercase alphanumerics with . _ - separators; immutable across versions. The manager keys enablement state and config off this.

version.description: Semantic version (semver.org). The manager uses it for upgrade ordering and to pin an enabled version.

displayName.description: Human label shown in the console Plugins pane, e.g. "Squawk Box".

description.description: One- or two-sentence plain-language summary for the catalog card. Optional.

kind.description: client = runs in/with the user-facing app (a UI surface, e.g. the Squawk Box walkie-talkie); service = a long-lived backend the manager launches and supervises (e.g. an SSH relay); bridge = connects Bard to an external system/protocol (e.g. a gateway to a third-party network). Determines how the manager treats `entry` and health.

requiredCapabilities.description: Fleet/device capabilities the plugin needs to function, drawn from the same vocabulary the Registry advertises per agent (e.g. "audio", "gpu", "llm", "network.lan"). Aligns with the power-profile capability axis: the manager only offers/enables this plugin on devices or workgroups whose advertised capabilities are a superset of this list. Empty = runs anywhere.

configSchema.description: A nested JSON Schema (draft 2020-12) describing the plugin's OWN configuration. The console renders a form from this and validates the operator's input against it before enabling. Opaque to the manager beyond being a valid schema — the plugin owns its config shape. Omit or {} for a plugin that takes no config.

healthEndpoint.description: Path the manager polls for liveness/readiness once enabled, e.g. "/healthz". null = no health surface (the manager shows the plugin as enabled but unmonitored — typical for a pure client UI plugin).

entry.description: How the plugin is launched or served. Exactly the launch detail the manager needs — interpretation depends on `kind`.

type.description: module = an importable Python/JS entrypoint the host loads in-process (typical client); container = an OCI image the manager runs under podman with the device's power-profile (typical service/bridge); url = an already-running endpoint the manager attaches to.

target.description: The thing to launch/attach: a module path (pkg.module:factory), an image ref (ghcr.io/edhaynes/bard-ssh:1.0.0), or a URL (https://host:port).

args.description: Optional launch arguments passed verbatim (e.g. container args, CLI flags).
